# Transcrição: 50. Trait 49 - Great Salespeople seek to dominate not compete.

- **Fonte:** 50. Trait 49 - Great Salespeople seek to dominate not compete.
- **Duração:** 01:22
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:22]

The greats seek to dominate, not compete. The greats are like competition. You're talking about competition as a sick word. To them, competition is like, oh my gosh, be a little baby, be a sissy. They're like, I don't want to compete. I want to dominate the sector. I want to own the sector. I'm not interested in competition, the greats say. I want to separate myself so far from the second, third, and fourth. I'm not even in the same race with them anymore. People have given up. You want to dominate your sector. You don't want to play fair. You don't want to be in a race. You want to be the only person on the field. Hey, they literally give you the win because nobody else shows up. That's not playing fair. You don't need to play fair. Let the politicians talk about how fair it is. Okay? Be ethical. You don't need to play fair. Be the only one in the field because you've done all the other things to separate yourself. Dominate. Own the space. Be the only player. Do what others refuse to do and you won't have to compete anymore. Think Coca-Cola. Google. Hopefully these companies are still here when this video plays. Think Coca-Cola, Google, Apple. Think about companies when they were in their greatness. Ford Motor Company, when they dominated the sector and they owned it. Think about America when it wants to dominate it as a world superpower. Dominate. Don't compete.
